reviews.author: Tia
reviews.text: Quan Ba Heaven Gate is truly breathtaking. Standing at this viewpoint and looking out over the valley is one of those moments where you naturally slow down and just take everything in. The layered mountains and patchwork of fields below create an incredibly beautiful landscape that feels even more impressive in person. It’s a peaceful place to stop, grab a coffee, and enjoy the fresh air and cooler temperature while soaking in the surrounding nature. The viewpoint is easy to access and well worth the stop if you’re travelling through the area. Photos don’t quite do it justice. Being there and seeing the scale of the scenery firsthand makes it a genuinely memorable experience and one I’d highly recommend.

reviews.author: Rosie Cole
reviews.text: Visited on last day of our 3 day ha giang loop, amazing views across 2 valleys. Also, obviously, coffee stops of either side of the road for your coconuts coffee needs ☕💪 Worth noting how incredibly busy it was on the other side of the viewpoint heading back to ha giang. The road is being repaired, there is gravel everywhere, buses, trucks, cars and 100s of motorbikes all meeting in narrow sections. See video for the carnage, all adds to the experience 😂 Apparently there was a large gate here erected by the French colonial rulers to keep the northern Vietnamese in their place. Lucky them having this amazing landscape and no invaders bothering them!

reviews.author: Srikanta Misra
reviews.text: Absolutely incredible experience of mountain and green. We stayed in a homestay. Food was incredible with vegetarian options & local wine available. The valley has a view of corn field, rice fields, water reservoirs. The homestay rooms were very well done, neat and clean with hot water provision. One can have a nice hot shower. There was also cultural program in the evening. A must visit.

reviews.author: Xuan Minh Nguyen
reviews.text: Quản Bạ is peaceful and beautiful, with fresh air and stunning mountain views — a perfect stop to slow down and enjoy nature. If you’re here, don’t miss Mr. Lực’s cassava cake nearby. It’s warm, soft, and simple, but absolutely delicious — a small local treat that makes the journey even more memorable. 🍠

reviews.author: Nuong
reviews.text: Beautiful place. You could view Tam Son town and Co Tien mountains

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: Stunning view! It is really the gate of heaven. On the way from here to Quan Ba, Dong Van, meo Vac, we can see how amazing the nature beauty is

reviews.author: Dj Ravermonkey
reviews.text: Very beautiful it is breathtaking and such a n beautiful experience to be at this viewpoint grab a coffee and take in the nature

reviews.author: Keng Wee
reviews.text: Great viewpoint, have a few cafes here to chill.

reviews.author: Enjoi
reviews.text: Nice viewpoint, where you can relax a bit with a drink.

reviews.author: Monica Tinna
reviews.text: 12 Mar 2024 We took videos and photos from the roadside in the coffee shop. The view was amazing. I am a travel agent and traveller You can contact me on my Instagram ID Personal ID: mystry_scrt Business ID: mercijourney

reviews.author: Sergio Marani
reviews.text: A breathtaking gateway to Hà Giang! Quản Bạ Heaven Gate is the first major viewpoint on the Hà Giang Loop, offering stunning panoramic views of the Tam Sơn valley and the famous Twin Mountains. The road leading here is steep and winding but well-paved, making the ride both thrilling and scenic. Located about 50 km from Hà Giang City, you can reach it by motorbike or car, following the QL4C road towards Đồng Văn. As you ascend, the air becomes cooler, and the lush green mountains stretch endlessly in every direction. Arriving at the top, the viewpoint provides a perfect spot to take in the breathtaking scenery and appreciate the start of Hà Giang’s dramatic landscapes. Stopping here is a must, not just for the view but also to experience the transition from the lower valleys to the rugged limestone peaks that define this incredible region.

reviews.author: Norwegian guide
reviews.text: Nice views. But there was nothing on the top of the stairs.

reviews.author: Nikolay Kamenov
reviews.text: Beware! This spot is not where Heaven Gate is on google maps. It is about 300 meters down the road.

reviews.author: Belle
reviews.text: This viewing point is good for taking photos. On the background, you can see the rice fields - flat and terraced, the hills and the silhouettes of the mountains farther behind, the road that snakes around the valleys, the blue skies and clouds. I learned a lot about this Geopark from the sign board, it's such an interesting and diverse place worthy of UNESCO's acknowledgment. There's also a cafe to get drinks and take a rest before exploring the rest of the loop.

reviews.author: RenOverSea
reviews.text: What a panoramic view over the majestic Dong Van Karst Plateau and its endless hills in the background from here on the Quan Ba Pass and Gateway into Dong Van, it makes the ride up here so worthwhile

reviews.author: Megaworld Asia
reviews.text: Heaven’s Gate: This is a bucket list stop on the Ha Giang Loop. It’s located 47 km north of Ha Giang City and is generally the first coffee stop for the tour groups. It’s got an outstanding view from the parking area and is worth stopping for. However, with the large numbers which are often there, you may have to wait for a while to get that all important selfie. If you aren’t impressed with the squadrons of bikes which are usually parked there, you can cross the road and hike up to a higher vantage point for much better views with no crowds. Safe travels, Mega.

reviews.author: Katia Shilov
reviews.text: Fantastic place with the best view on loop

reviews.author: Brandy Urstadt
reviews.text: beautiful view and great coffee! i got an iced coffee, perfect for a recharge after being on the bike for awhile.

reviews.author: Luc Diefenbach
reviews.text: Wow, what a beautiful site to take in this place was really the heaven gate! It was so vast and breathtaking coming up here by motorbike is awesome and sitting at one of the shops drinking a coffee and looking out is an immaculate vibe.

reviews.author: Tầm Mạc Văn
reviews.text: Tourist Stop Place. Tourist can stop for resting and enjoy some drinks. Nice view from here to National Road 4C. Sometime road looks like as a snake hidden in cloud, so fantastic!

reviews.author: Malayshree
reviews.text: Nice lookout place with full view of valley. Need to climb almost 100 steep stairs to reach at the top.

reviews.author: devabrat bindhani
reviews.text: Quan Ba Heaven Gate, also known as Quan Ba Pass or Quan Ba Twin Mountains, is a scenic mountain pass located in Quan Ba district, Ha Giang province, Vietnam. It is part of the famous Dong Van Karst Plateau Geopark, which is recognized as a UNESCO Global Geopark. The Heaven Gate is named after the stunning panoramic views it offers. As you ascend the pass, you will be greeted by breathtaking vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys. The highlight of the area is the twin mountains, also known as the "Fairy Bosom" due to their resemblance to a pair of breasts. These twin peaks have become an iconic symbol of Quan Ba and are often photographed by visitors. The journey to Quan Ba Heaven Gate is an adventure in itself, as you drive through winding roads, passing through small villages and terraced fields. The landscape is characterized by limestone formations, rugged cliffs, and lush greenery, creating a picturesque backdrop for your journey. At the Heaven Gate, you can stop to take photos, enjoy the cool mountain air, and soak in the natural beauty of the area. There are also small vendors selling local handicrafts and souvenirs. Quan Ba Heaven Gate is a popular destination for nature lovers, photographers, and adventure enthusiasts. It offers a unique opportunity to explore the stunning landscapes and experience the charm of the Ha Giang region in northern Vietnam.

reviews.author: Neil Pinto
reviews.text: 360° views of the prettiest of towns surrounded by the lushest if green mountains. Heavens Gate could very well be, if you take the 'Stairway to Heaven'.🤣😂😍

reviews.author: Edwin Sim
reviews.text: A spectacular view of the mountains and it's skylines.

reviews.author: Alicja
reviews.text: Picturesque place to stay for a while and enjoy the view. Very pleasant place. There are few cafes in the neighborhood so you can drink hot tea or coffee (I was very cold and windy when I was there). Nothing has remained in relation to the gate itself but the view will reward your trip.

reviews.author: Karla du Toit
reviews.text: One of the prettiest places. There is a tiny shop where you can order a beverage and just sit and enjoy the view. Great place for a pit stop on the ha giang loop.

reviews.author: A. DK.
reviews.text: Nice view stop. Especially if you're already passing by. You can go up the steps behind the viewpoint to the tower and there is nothing to stop you from climbing right on up to the top except the fact that the stairs are very unstable past the first level. There is a couple places to grab a drink or snack. No charge for view or parking.

reviews.author: Jonas Mustermann
reviews.text: Just a view Point. The view is very nice but there are a Lots of nice views in ha giang. There is Not a really Special thing for me

reviews.author: Paul Parent
reviews.text: Positioned at 1,500 m above sea level, Quan Ba Heaven Gate sits along National Highway 4C, about 43–50 km north of Hà Giang city and roughly 3 km from Tam Sơn Town The journey to get there is part of the adventure: the road is a thrilling mountain pass with steep cliffs on one side and deep ravines on the other. Traveling by motorbike is the most common way, offering flexibility to pause and take in the views It’s also accessible by private car or via tour arrangements, though the terrain still demands care From the vantage point of the gate, the Tam Sơn Valley unfolds below—a patchwork of terraced fields, karst hills, and ethnic. On clear days, the view stretches far; early morning or late afternoon brings especially beautiful lighting .

reviews.author: Sochu Yuzu
reviews.text: It is near Yen Minh town, we can have nice pictures here. Feeling driving on winding hill is so great, but be careful with truck 😊 from here we can see a double mountains that arranged in breast shape. Come and discover yourself.
